page: Microsoft Azure E64_v5 is an Ev5-family memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base frequency of 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by a cache hierarchy of 48 KB L1d, 1 MB L2, and 48 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieves a strong multi-core stress-ng score of 62,127.65 and a Passmark CPU score of 60,629.99, alongside a strong Geekbench multi-core score of 16,157.0. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient for large blocks, reaching 286.33 GB/sec for read-only and 130.34 GB/sec for write operations. Multi-threaded operations perform exceptionally well, as demonstrated by a bzip3 decompression speed of 296.62 GB/sec and a Passmark database operations score of 30,366.56. However, single-core metrics, such as the Geekbench single-core score of 1,638.0 and stress-ng single-core performance of 1,899.08, fall into the average tier. Large-scale LLM inference is a notable bottleneck, with 70B model text generation performing weakly at 2.74 tokens/sec.

Given its high memory density and dedicated CPU allocation, the E64_v5 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The absence of bundled local storage means storage costs must be managed externally, which represents a tradeoff for IOPS-heavy local database setups. It is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, including large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, enterprise resource planning systems, and multi-threaded data processing pipelines.
description: The Microsoft Azure E64_v5 is an Ev5-family memory-optimized virtual machine configured with 64 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB of memory per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, it features 32 physical cores with hyper-threading and a 48 MB L3 cache. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, with a Passmark CPU score of 60,629.99 and strong large-block memory bandwidth, though single-core performance and large LLM inference speeds are average to weak. This profile makes the E64_v5 highly efficient for memory-intensive, multi-threaded workloads such as relational databases, in-memory data stores, and heavy data processing pipelines where local storage is not a prerequisite.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64_v5 is an Ev5-family memory-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB of RAM, and strong multi-core performance suited for large database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64_v5 is a memory-optimized server with 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 512 GB RAM, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized virtual machine featuring sixty-four dedicated cores and half a terabyte of RAM for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8370C processors
512.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
Strong multi-core performance and large-block memory bandwidth
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching, and data processing
